conclusion: Insufficient matching data for Zapier. Career page has 18 jobs; Linkedin has 7 jobs. No cross-platform matches found — titles may differ significantly or Linkedin snapshots are sparse.
geminiSummary:
For Zapier, the analysis between January 1, 2024, and April 1, 2026, revealed a striking lack of overlap in job postings across its career page and LinkedIn. Crucially, zero job postings were matched across both platforms during the observation period. This means it is not possible to determine if Zapier's career page precedes LinkedIn for job postings, nor by what average or median lag, as there were no shared jobs to compare. Of the 18 unique jobs identified on Zapier's career page, all 18 were exclusively found there, never appearing on LinkedIn. Conversely, 7 unique jobs were found on LinkedIn but not on the career page within the same timeframe.

The complete absence of matched jobs means there is no lag distribution to interpret. Instead, this finding strongly suggests that for Zapier, job postings on their company career page are entirely distinct from those found on LinkedIn, at least under the current analysis parameters. All 18 jobs present on Zapier's career page were classified as "career page only," indicating that relying solely on LinkedIn would have caused a job seeker to miss every single one of these opportunities.

Several caveats are essential when interpreting these findings. The primary limitation is the fundamental lack of matched job postings, which prevents any conclusions about lead times. The snapshot frequency for LinkedIn (70 processed) was also significantly lower than for the career page (200 processed), potentially contributing to the difficulty in finding matches or a lower representation of Zapier's jobs on LinkedIn. Despite an extensive analysis period, the relatively low number of unique jobs found on LinkedIn (7) further suggests that Zapier's primary job advertising strategy, as captured by this data, may not heavily involve LinkedIn.

Given that 100% of the jobs found on Zapier's career page during this analysis never appeared on LinkedIn, the practical recommendation for job seekers is unequivocal: always monitor Zapier's career page directly. Relying on third-party platforms like LinkedIn would result in missing all job opportunities exclusively posted on their company site.
